explanation: This command uses the flags -c and -s to clear and display the system event log. It matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria by including the -c flag and referencing the 'system' log.
This command exports the application event log as an evt file using the -g flag. It aligns with the Sigma rule by referencing the 'application' log and using a flagged command-line option.
This command displays the security event log using the -d flag. It meets the Sigma rule's criteria by referencing the 'security' log and using a flagged command-line option.
This command displays the system event log in XML format using the -x flag. It aligns with the Sigma rule by referencing the 'system' log and using a flagged command-line option.
This command clears and displays the security event log using the -c and -d flags. It matches the Sigma rule's criteria by referencing the 'security' log and using flagged command-line options.
